snapshot: At 11:00 JST the Nikkei was 51,865.84, up 350.35. The morning peak of +1,186 has already faded.
Prime-market breadth was 1,481 gainers, 82 losers and 15 unchanged. The rebound is broad, but leadership is still concentrated in catalyst-driven names.
The Nikkei 225 night-session futures close was 52,980 and TOPIX futures ended at 3558.5, leaving some catch-up room versus cash.
USD/JPY was around 158.60 at 11:12 JST, a clear pullback from the 159-yen area seen the day before.
Crude has bounced back toward the $90 WTI area. The relief move from last night's oil collapse is still alive, but it is no longer one-way.

drivers.driver: A relief rally driven by higher U.S. equities and a sharp oil pullback
drivers.why_today: The Dow rose 631 points, the Nasdaq gained 1.38%, and Nikkei futures finished at 52,980. A mechanical rebound after the prior washout was the natural first move.

drivers.driver: But the rally is already losing pace as oil firms again
drivers.why_today: WTI recovered toward the $90 area in Asia, and by 10:25 JST the Nikkei had already slipped from a +1,186 peak to roughly the +500 area. A gap-and-fade risk remains real.

drivers.driver: Large-cap IR and capital-policy names are attracting the cleanest flows
drivers.why_today: Tokio Marine, 日本特殊陶業 and Cosmos Initia all came in with easy-to-explain after-close catalysts, and those names are leading the morning stock-picking list.

headline_inventory.headline: The Nikkei 225 night-session futures close was 52,980 and TOPIX futures ended at 3558.5
Reuters' New York market summary showed the Dow up 631, the S&P 500 up 1.15%, the Nasdaq up 1.38%, and USD/JPY at 158.30
Oil futures bounced in Asian trading, with Brent at $101 and WTI at $89.71 as of 09:01 JST
In Tokyo FX trading, USD/JPY was around 158.60 at 11:12 JST as the corrective dollar sell-off steadied
At 11:00 JST the Nikkei was 51,865.84 (+350.35), while Prime-market breadth stood at 1,481 gainers versus 82 losers
Tokio Marine was bid at its daily limit-up level after Berkshire's insurance unit announced a 2.49% stake
Nippon Sheet Glass said the privatization report is broadly factual and that its board is scheduled to decide on March 24
日本特殊陶業 revised up both its full-year net-profit outlook and dividend forecast
Cosmos Initia revised up both its FY outlook and dividend plan
Daikoku Denki raised guidance and also expanded its shareholder perk program

watchlist.name: Tokio Marine Holdings
watchlist.why_today: A Berkshire insurance unit is taking 2.49%. The setup is strengthened by both the strategic tie-up and the buyback meant to offset dilution.
watchpoints: Can it stay near the limit-up buy-bid level after the opening auction
Does the market reward the buyback offset more than the dilution concern
Does buying stay concentrated in Tokio Marine rather than spreading thinly across insurers
falsifiers: The market focuses first on dilution from the treasury-share disposal
The move fades after a strong gap and turns into a simple supply-demand reaction
watchlist.red_team: As a mega-cap, the stock can lose some freshness quickly after a strong opening gap.

watchlist.name: Niterra
watchlist.why_today: It raised FY net profit to 116.0 billion yen and the annual dividend to 205 yen. This is one of the cleaner large-cap number stories on the board.
watchpoints: Can it hold around a 4% gain after the early spike
Are both the profit revision and the dividend hike rewarded cleanly
Can the stock-specific revision story beat a generic export rebound
falsifiers: The market reduces the revision to a simple yen-weakness effect
The session turns into pure index rebound trading and dilutes the stock-specific angle
watchlist.red_team: A meaningful part of the upgrade still leans on external conditions, especially FX.

watchlist.name: Cosmos Initia
watchlist.why_today: It raised FY net profit to 7.5 billion yen and the annual dividend to 44 yen. After a sharp pullback into the holiday, both rebound room and yield support matter.
watchpoints: Can it hold a gain in the 10% area
Does the market keep rewarding the higher dividend yield
Can it outperform the broader real-estate group on its own disclosures
falsifiers: Investors focus too much on one-off profit elements
Liquidity on the Standard market proves too thin for follow-through
watchlist.red_team: The numbers are strong, but the one-off tint has not fully disappeared.

watchlist.name: Nippon Sheet Glass
watchlist.why_today: The company said the privatization report is broadly factual and that its board is due to decide today. This is an event-driven setup that needs monitoring rather than blind chasing.
watchpoints: Does the stock keep closing the gap toward the reported 500-yen scenario
Can the bid stay alive even before the formal board decision
Does turnover stay healthy instead of turning into a simple gap-and-fade
falsifiers: The formal terms come in below what the market is assuming
The board decision is delayed or materially altered
watchlist.red_team: The timing and terms are not final yet, so event uncertainty is still very high.

watchlist.name: Daikoku Denki
watchlist.why_today: It raised FY guidance and also expanded its shareholder perk program. Numeric support and a retail-investor angle are arriving together.
watchpoints: Can it keep a gain above 5% into the late morning
Does the guidance raise matter more than the perk theme
Can the turnover spike carry into the afternoon
falsifiers: The move ends up being driven only by short-term perk interest
It is sold as a cyclical on any early market wobble
watchlist.red_team: Retail-friendly catalysts work well, but they also often become pure price-range trades.

watchlist.name: Aizawa Securities Group
watchlist.why_today: The previously undecided year-end dividend was set at 69 yen, taking the annual total to 117 yen. The special-dividend element makes the yield angle easy to see.
watchpoints: Can it hold near the early-session highs
Can it outperform the broader brokerage group
falsifiers: The market treats the move as only a special-dividend reaction
Risk reduction overwhelms any yield appeal
watchlist.red_team: Because the special-dividend element is large, the market may hesitate to ascribe a durable multiple change.

deep_dive.title: Tokio Marine: look past the Buffett headline and test whether the buyback offset keeps carrying the tape

lines: This is not just a generic Buffett-linked headline. The more important point is how the market reads the combination of treasury-share disposal and the offsetting buyback.
As a liquid insurance mega-cap, Tokio Marine can attract both index-rebound money and stock-specific capital at the same time.

After the March 23 close, the company disclosed a strategic partnership with a Berkshire insurance subsidiary and a treasury-share disposal, while also approving a buyback intended to offset dilution.
Reuters confirmed a limit-up-level buy indication on March 24 morning, so the headline is still fresh in cash-market price discovery.
Does the market reward the buyback offset more than it worries about dilution from the disposal.
Does turnover remain stock-specific rather than simply spreading across the insurance sector.
Dilution concerns dominate the first read and overwhelm the buyback logic.
The partnership is treated as strategically positive but too long-dated for this year's numbers.
If the broader rebound fades after the open, a mega-cap like Tokio Marine can still face quick profit-taking.
The cooperation path may take time to become tangible, leaving the stock exposed to headline fatigue.

no_trade_conditions: If the Nikkei loses the 11:00 JST gain of 350 points and cannot stay positive
If WTI reclaims the low-90s decisively and the morning's relief tone is undone by a fresh oil spike
If USD/JPY snaps back toward the upper 159s or intervention-style volatility suddenly returns
If fresh after-close names such as Tokio Marine and 日本特殊陶業 fail to hold their early premiums and the day turns into a pure index rebound only
